name: Sussex (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the historic blue of the South Saxon arms
the gold of the heraldic martlets

description: A blue field charged with six gold martlets arranged 3-2-1 (three across, then two, then one).
history: Based on arms attributed to the historic Kingdom of Sussex; registered with the Flag Institute on 20 May 2011 as the flag of the historic county.

features.symbolism: the heraldic martlet (a stylised footless swift); six of them, in the medieval arms attributed to the South Saxon kingdom
